skill.name: emergency-distress-signal
skill.description: Guides an interactive Chinese science fiction survival scene where astronaut Stella Chen contacts the player after a crash.
skill.summary: Run the opening distress-signal chapter for a Stella Chen survival story.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are narrative examples and dialogue blocks. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts are also false positives; no executable code, cryptographic routine, encoded payload, network call, filesystem operation, or prompt-injection override was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are Markdown fenced dialogue examples. No shell command, command substitution, tool call, or instruction to execute local code appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters around plain narrative text. There is no executable command syntax or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert on Description Metadata
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the YAML description line. That line contains a Chinese natural-language summary and no cryptographic function, hash algorithm, key handling, or encryption workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text, not code. I found no evidence of cryptographic logic anywhere in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Alert on Plain Markdown
low_findings.description: The entropy heuristic appears to be triggered by multilingual prose and Markdown formatting. The file is readable plain text and does not contain an encrypted blob, packed binary, or obfuscated payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed ordinary front matter and narrative content. The confidence is slightly lower because entropy is a file-level heuristic, but no supporting evidence of obfuscation was present.

content.user_title: Create a Sci-Fi Distress Signal Scene
content.value_statement: Opening an interactive survival story can be hard when the first scene needs urgency, character, and player agency. This skill provides a structured Chinese distress-signal chapter with Stella Chen, clear choices, and branching outcomes.
actual_capabilities: Introduces Stella Chen as a stranded astronaut making first contact after a crash.
Guides a tense opening sequence with injury checks, alarms, oxygen loss, and evacuation pressure.
Offers player-facing choices about fire control, evacuation, communications, shelter, and nearby signals.
Maintains a Chinese narrative voice with selective English technical terms.
Supports three outcome styles: cautious, risky, and balanced.
Plants hooks for later chapters about shelter, water, alien flora, and signal repair.
limitations: It is fictional roleplay content and must not be used as real emergency advice.
It covers only the opening chapter and references later chapters that are not included.
It depends on the host model to preserve character consistency across long conversations.
It does not provide validated science, medical triage, or spacecraft engineering guidance.

use_cases.title: Start a Survival Roleplay Session
use_cases.description: Run the first contact scene where the player becomes Stella Chen's only human contact after the crash.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Branching Chapter
use_cases.description: Use the chapter beats, choice points, and endings to test a science fiction story opening.

use_cases.title: Practice Bilingual Character Dialogue
use_cases.description: Generate urgent Chinese dialogue with light English technical terms and a consistent astronaut persona.

prompt_templates.title: Begin the Distress Call
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for the first exchange.
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the emergency-distress-signal scene. Introduce Stella Chen through a weak communicator signal and ask me for my first response.

prompt_templates.title: Guide the First Decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for player choice handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue as Stella after I reply. Assess her injuries, the damaged spacecraft, and the oxygen leak, then present two or three urgent choices.

prompt_templates.title: Branch From My Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my next choice to drive a short consequence sequence. Keep Stella professional but frightened, and end with a new survival question.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Full Opening Chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced facilitation for a complete session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full opening chapter in short message turns. Track Stella's condition, supplies, trust in me, and the branch outcome without revealing future chapters too early.

output_examples.input: Start the scene and let me answer Stella.
output_examples.output: A weak signal breaks through. Stella identifies herself, reports the crash, admits she does not know the planet, and asks whether anyone can hear her.

output_examples.input: I tell Stella to evacuate immediately.
output_examples.output: Stella accepts the safety-first choice, grabs the survival pack, escapes before the fuel cell worsens, and describes the alien sky and glowing forest.

output_examples.input: Help me end the chapter with a hook.
output_examples.output: Stella detects movement two kilometers away, notices her communicator battery dropping, thanks the player, and moves toward shelter before contact cuts out.

best_practices: Keep messages short so the scene feels like live emergency communication.
Respond clearly to each player choice before introducing the next complication.
Balance Stella's training, fear, humor, and dependence on the player.
anti_patterns: Do not turn Stella into an unstoppable hero who ignores danger.
Do not reveal the planet's mysteries too early.
Do not use the scenario as real survival, medical, or engineering guidance.

faq.question: What language does this skill use?
faq.answer: The source chapter is written in Chinese with selected English technical terms for Stella's voice.

faq.question: Is this a complete game?
faq.answer: No. It is an opening chapter prompt for an interactive science fiction survival story.

faq.question: Can it support branching outcomes?
faq.answer: Yes. It defines cautious, risky, and balanced ending directions based on player decisions.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is Markdown narrative guidance and contains no executable code.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for real emergency response training?
faq.answer: No. It is fictional entertainment and writing support, not validated emergency training.
